message:
repair/core: add adaptive symbol-refresh protocol and Bayesian overhead autopilot

Introduces a full repair refresh-policy pipeline that supports eager, lazy, and adaptive refresh decisions per block group, plus explicit dirty/staleness tracking and policy-aware refresh triggers.

Detailed changes:

- Add new  with Bayesian durability autopilot primitives, posterior updates, risk-bound evaluation, and overhead-selection support types used by the repair pipeline.

- Extend  with  and group refresh state management, including write-triggered dirty marking, scrub-time refresh execution, adaptive threshold logic, staleness timeout forcing, and structured tracing/warn paths for policy decisions.

- Add/extend pipeline tests for eager refresh-on-write, lazy refresh-on-scrub, adaptive mode switching, and staleness-timeout behavior to keep refresh semantics deterministic and auditable.

- Tighten  degradation FSM behavior and test coverage: explicit ordering-match transitions, policy notification scoping, richer Debug output, and comprehensive unit tests for level ordering, escalation/de-escalation, backpressure gate behavior, and pressure-monitor integration.

Rationale: this commit groups the repair refresh-policy protocol and the core pressure-control test hardening that validates policy-driven behavior under load/degradation states.
